name: Kailua Beach Park
tagline: The beach Honolulu locals make the drive for — powder-white sand, gentle clear water, views of the Mokes.
parking: 3 free lots — arrive before 9am on weekends
bestTime: Arrive before 9am weekends; weekday mornings are uncrowded
talkStory: Kailua means 'two seas' or 'two currents' in Hawaiian, referring to the twin channels flanking the bay. President Obama repeatedly vacationed here, putting this once-sleepy local beach on the global map. Kayak to Moku Nui (the larger Mokulua Island) and hike over the ridge to a hidden back-side beach with zero people. The afternoon trade winds turn the bay into one of the world's best kiteboarding spots — even if you don't kite, the aerial show from shore is worth staying for.
